verb_relations.sentence:
4 Discussion
The complete response rates of the first-line young patients receiving high-dose chemotherapy with cytarabine and anthracycline are in the range of 50%-60% on intermediate risk disease and up to 80% on favourable risk disease, while in adverse risk disease is just around 40%.

verb_relations.subject: combination
verb_relations.sentence: Besides that, the combination of chemotherapy and new targeted drugs such as FLT3 inhibitors, particularly the addition of midostaurin, increases moderately the overall survival.

verb_relations.subject: addition
verb_relations.sentence: Furthermore, the addition of monoclonal antibody anti-CD33 drug conjugate (gemtuzumab-ozogamicin) also increases the overall survival and decreases the risk of relapse in favourable and intermediate risk.

verb_relations.subject: treatment
verb_relations.sentence: On the other hand, treatment with HMA in patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy have a CR rate of 28%.

verb_relations.subject: combination
verb_relations.sentence: It is important to highlight that the combination of BCL-2 inhibitor (venetoclax) with an HMA has registered better results than the standard of care in patients with adverse risk disease.

verb_relations.subject: combination
verb_relations.sentence: Recently, the combination of HMA and venetoclax in patients with adverse-risk cytogenetics and those at least 75 years old showed overall response rates of 60% and 65%, respectively (26).

verb_relations.subject: it
verb_relations.sentence: Nonetheless, it is important to consider the treatment line to assess the ICI efficacy profile.

verb_relations.subject: cases
verb_relations.sentence: While patients with R/R AML might present lower response rates, newly diagnosed cases typically exhibit higher response rates.

verb_relations.subject: we
verb_relations.sentence: In this review, we have seen another prove of this rationale when comparing ORR (62% vs. 33% for first line and R/R) ( Figure 4 ).
Gojo et al. reported a 14% CR/CRi in the R/R setting and a 47% CR/CRi in newly diagnosed patients using pembrolizumab, a PD-1 inhibitor, in combination with 5-azacytidine.
In this line, Sallman et al. (23) described an example of beneficial effect of ICI in early lines by testing the combination of Hu5F9-G4 (5F9), an antibody targeting CD47, with azacytidine.

On the contrary, Borate et al. (24) reported no differences in overall response rate between first-line and R/R patients when using MBG453, a TIM-3 inhibitor, in combination with decitabine (29% both groups).

verb_relations.subject: that
verb_relations.sentence: Furthermore, it was reported a CR/CRi in R/R that doubled the first-line rate (29% vs. 14%).

verb_relations.subject: results
verb_relations.sentence: These results, although controversial, may suggest TIM-3 as a therapeutic target, especially in the R/R, and support further clinical trials of MBG453 in combination with HMAs in patients with AML (24).

verb_relations.subject: majority
verb_relations.sentence: While most of these response rates may represent an indication of the efficacy of these therapies in the treatment of AML, it should not be overlooked that the vast majority of these studies used these therapies in combination with chemotherapy and/or allo-HST.

verb_relations.subject: AML
verb_relations.sentence: As reported by Castle et al. AML shows one of the lowest tumor mutational burdens across oncologic conditions (30).

verb_relations.subject: rates
verb_relations.sentence: Consequently, it provides possible justification as to why the response rates to these treatments are so limited in AML.

verb_relations.subject: mutations
verb_relations.sentence: As relatively few mutations take place in AML patients, low levels of neoantigens are generated, preventing these patients from benefiting from ICI therapy.

verb_relations.subject: burden
verb_relations.sentence: In other words, high tumor mutational burden can guide response to immunotherapy treatment.

verb_relations.subject: who
verb_relations.sentence: To successfully use this kind of therapies, standardized biomarkers are needed to help predict response and facilitate the selection of patients who may benefit from its use (31).

verb_relations.subject: need
verb_relations.sentence: Also, as new checkpoint inhibitors emerge, the need to further understand the efficacy profile of these approaches increases.

verb_relations.subject: Relationships
verb_relations.sentence: Relationships between ICI mechanisms of action and genetic profiles may provide further information as where and when to use these therapies (32).

verb_relations.subject: who
verb_relations.sentence: Nevertheless, due to the small number of patients who suffered irAEs, further studies are needed to establish this relationship.

verb_relations.subject: Immunotherapy
verb_relations.sentence:
5 Conclusion
Immunotherapy based on checkpoint inhibitors in combination with intensive chemotherapy, hypomethylating agents or other targeted therapies is gaining interest in the management of hematological malignancies such as AML.

verb_relations.subject: who
verb_relations.sentence: Hopefully, prospective study of responses to this type of treatments according to different biological profiles might provide strategies to identify those patients who may benefit from ICI.

verb_relations.subject: most
verb_relations.sentence: Nonetheless, it is important to highlight the limitations of this review where most of the studies were early phase trials.

verb_relations.subject: that
verb_relations.sentence: Also, most of included studies were reported as congress abstracts, potentially missing relevant information that could affect this meta-analysis results.

verb_relations.subject: JCM

verb_relations.sentence: They are among those virulence factors that destroy connective tissue proteins, cytokines, cell membranes, and antibodies and modulate P. aeruginosa infections in proper sites such as burned skin or cystic fibrosis lungs.

verb_relations.sentence: Besides these virulence factors that make microorganism a destructive pathogen, antibiotic resistance also complicates the treatment of P. aeruginosa infections.

2.4 Primary Cilia
Primary cilia is widely found in osteocytes, MC3T3-E1 cells, murine long bone osteocyte-Y4 (MLO-Y4) osteoid cells, cranial osteoblasts, and hMSCs, and it is an important mechanoreceptor that responds to mechanical stimulation and coordinated load induction in these cells (Xiao et al., 2006; Malone et al., 2007; Hoey et al., 2012).

3.1.5 Fluid Shear Stress
Fluid shear stress (FSS) is a type of mechanical stress caused by extracellular fluid, such as tissue fluid, flowing through the cell membrane surface, and the theory that FSS is induced by the flow of tissue fluid in the lacunar-canalicular system is widely accepted (Kumar et al., 2021).

3.1.5 Fluid Shear Stress
Fluid shear stress (FSS) is a type of mechanical stress caused by extracellular fluid, such as tissue fluid, flowing through the cell membrane surface, and the theory that FSS is induced by the flow of tissue fluid in the lacunar-canalicular system is widely accepted (Kumar et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: Osteoclasts
verb_relations.sentence: Osteoclasts and osteoblasts play a major role in bone tissue homeostasis.

verb_relations.subject: osteoblasts
verb_relations.sentence: Osteoclasts mainly play a role in bone resorption, whereas osteoblasts are mainly involved in bone remodeling processes, such as bone cell formation, mineralization, and secretion.

verb_relations.subject: types
verb_relations.sentence: These cell types balance and restrict each other to maintain bone tissue metabolism.

verb_relations.subject: stress
verb_relations.sentence: Consequently, mechanical stress exerts an important influence on the bone microenvironment and bone metabolism.

verb_relations.subject: article
verb_relations.sentence: This article highlights the involvement of osteoclasts and osteoblasts in activating different mechanical transduction pathways and reports changings in their differentiation, formation, and functional mechanism induced by the application of different types of mechanical stress to bone tissue.

verb_relations.subject: review
verb_relations.sentence: This review could provide new ideas for further microscopic studies of bone health, disease, and tissue damage reconstruction.

verb_relations.subject: forces
verb_relations.sentence:
Keywords: mechanical stress, stimulation, function, expression mechanism, osteoblasts
1 Introduction
Mechanical forces affect almost every sphere of various life processes of living organisms, such as the perception of external hearing and touch, fluid flow and deformation during embryonic development, changes in cell osmotic pressure, pressure on blood vessel walls, and the movement of individual animals regulated by the earth’s gravitational environment.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: These forces range from mechanical stress signal generation, induction, and transduction to the final response, which involves the cell membrane, cytoderm, cytoskeleton, and other structures.

verb_relations.subject: stress
verb_relations.sentence: Consequently, mechanical stress exerts an important influence on the bone microenvironment and metabolism.

verb_relations.subject: load
verb_relations.sentence: In contrast, the mechanical load caused by exercise can restore bone mass and reverse these effects in most situations (Iura et al., 2015; Suniaga et al., 2018).

verb_relations.subject: Exposure
verb_relations.sentence: Exposure of tissues and cells to external mechanical stress transforms the external mechanical force into local mechanical signals in the body, triggering responses of cellular sensors.

verb_relations.subject: Osteoblasts
verb_relations.sentence: Osteoblasts, osteocytes, bone lining cells, osteoclasts, and macrophages can sense mechanical stimulation and respond directly or indirectly (Dong et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: channels
verb_relations.sentence: Ion channels, integrins, gap junction proteins, focal adhesion kinase, the extracellular matrix, the cellular skeletal components (such as intermediate filaments, microtubules, and actin filaments), and primary cilia are mechanical sensors that have been proven to regulate intracellular signaling pathways (Qin et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: studies
verb_relations.sentence: In vitro studies often use peripheral blood mononuclear cells, monocyte cells, bone marrow derived precursors and RAW264.7 cells induced to exhibit osteoclast formation (Owen and Reilly, 2018; Xiang et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: Osteocytes
verb_relations.sentence: Osteocytes, osteoclasts and osteoblasts play major roles in bone tissue homeostasis.

verb_relations.subject: RANKL
verb_relations.sentence: RANKL secreted by osteocytes binds to the receptor RANK on the precursor surface of osteoclasts to promote the differentiation and maturation of osteoclasts.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: Osteocytes also secrete OPG, which acts as the decoy receptor of RANKL and negatively regulates RANK signal to prevent osteoclast differentiation.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: In addition, osteocytes secrete sclerotin, which is a negative regulator of bone formation (Bonucci, 2009; Prideaux et al., 2016).

verb_relations.subject: types
verb_relations.sentence: These cell types balance and restrict each other to maintain bone tissue metabolism and homeostasis.

verb_relations.subject: stimulation
verb_relations.sentence: Previous studies have shown that appropriate mechanical stress stimulation can reduce the number and activity of osteoclasts and inhibit bone resorption, promote the differentiation and osteogenic function of osteoblasts, inhibit the differentiation of BMSCs into adipocytes, and prevent the loss of bone mass (Uzbekov et al., 2012).

verb_relations.subject: effect
verb_relations.sentence: This effect also critically influences the regulation of bone metabolism signaling pathways (Uzbekov et al., 2012; Kameyama et al., 2013).

verb_relations.subject: studies
verb_relations.sentence: Numerous studies have further investigated the mechanism underlying the effects of mechanical stress on bone metabolism by examining mechanical stress stimulation in osteoclasts and osteoblasts (involving different species such as humans, mice and zebrafish,etc.) (Nomura and Takano-Yamamoto, 2000; Ho et al., 2005; Wittkowske et al., 2016).

verb_relations.subject: we
verb_relations.sentence: In this review, we review the mechanisms of mechanical stress stimulation on the function and expression of osteoblasts.

verb_relations.subject: we
verb_relations.sentence: Through this review, we attempt to provide a theoretical basis for the microscopic study of bone health, diseases, and injury reconstruction.

verb_relations.subject: stimulation
verb_relations.sentence:
2 Mechanoreceptor
2.1 Ion Channels
Appropriate mechanical stimulation can activate calcium channels on the cell membrane to promote the transport of extracellular calcium into the cell, increasing the intracellular calcium concentration and promoting bone mass increase.

verb_relations.subject: deficiency
verb_relations.sentence: Piezo1 expression in osteoblasts may also be promoted by mechanical tensile force (Wang et al., 2020a) and its deficiency in osteoblasts promotes bone resorption and contributes to osteoporosis in mice, but does not affect bone mass (Wang et al., 2020b).

verb_relations.subject: removal
verb_relations.sentence: Li et al. (Li et al., 2019) reported that the removal of Piezo1 from osteoblasts and bone cells does not completely eliminate the response of bones to mechanical stimuli.

verb_relations.subject: cytoskeleton
verb_relations.sentence:
Other channels, such as the transient receptor potential (TRP) vanilloid (TRPV) and certain members of the epithelial Na+ channel (ENaC) protein family, can guide cation influx under a hypertonic environment or membrane tension, converting mechanical force signals into electrical and chemical signals (Gu and Gu, 2014; Kefauver et al., 2020).
2.2 Cytoskeleton
The cytoskeleton is a network structure in cells that is mainly composed of protein fiber (Kounakis and Tavernarakis, 2019), and mainly consists of microtubules, actin fibers, and intermediate filaments (Sandbo et al., 2016).

verb_relations.subject: It
verb_relations.sentence: It plays an important role in maintaining cell morphology, bearing external forces, and maintaining the internal cellular structure.

verb_relations.subject: cytoskeleton
verb_relations.sentence: The cytoskeletal structure is highly nonlinear, enabling cells to sense deformation and change and the complete cytoskeleton contributes to maintaining tight adhesion between cells and the extracellular matrix (ECM).

verb_relations.subject: family
verb_relations.sentence: The integrin glycoprotein family located on the cell membrane senses mechanical signals through interactions between the ECM and intracellular signals (Aisha et al., 2015).

verb_relations.subject: that
verb_relations.sentence: Microtubule actin cross-linking factor 1 (MACF1) is a regulator of cytoskeletal dynamics that is necessary for maintaining bone tissue integrity (Wang et al., 2021a), whereas actin in the cytoskeleton is mainly involved in mechanical stress (Zhou et al., 2018).

verb_relations.subject: link
verb_relations.sentence: The link between mechanical stimulation, integrin/cytoskeleton/Scr/extracellular signal-regulated protein kinase (ERK) signaling pathway activation, and osteocyte survival provides a mechanical basis for the role of mechanical forces in the bone (Plotkin et al., 2005).

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence:
Integrin
Integrins are heterodimers formed by the non-covalent binding of α and β subunits and presently, mammals are known to express18 α and 8 β subunits, which combine to form 24 integrins (Selvakumarasamy et al., 2019).

verb_relations.subject: Integrin
verb_relations.sentence: Integrin senses physical or biochemical stimulation of the ECM by binding to its ligands (including fibonectin, collagen, and laminin) in the extracellular region.

verb_relations.subject: integrin
verb_relations.sentence: Furthermore, through conformational changes, integrin mediates the transmission of signals to cells to induce their adhesion, migration, proliferation, and differentiation.

verb_relations.subject: changes
verb_relations.sentence: Moreover, intracellular signal changes also affect the conformational changes of integrin, alter the affinity of ligand binding, and affect the biological behavior of cells.

verb_relations.subject: mechanism
verb_relations.sentence: This integrin-dependent bidirectional signal transduction mechanism plays an important role in bone remodeling (Uda et al., 2017; Kong et al., 2020; Michael and Parsons, 2020).

verb_relations.subject: osteoclasts
verb_relations.sentence: Recent studies have shown that osteoclasts express integrin α2 and αV and play an important role in bone resorption (Kong et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: studies
verb_relations.sentence: Subsequent studies should focus on further clarifying the important role of integrin in mechanical stimulation and bone metabolism.

verb_relations.subject: exposure
verb_relations.sentence: The exposure of osteoblasts and hMSCs to suitable FSS upregulated the expression levels of runt-related transcription factor 2 (RUNX2), bone morphogenetic protein 2 (BMP2), alkaline phosphatase (ALP), and osteopontin (Sonam et al., 2016).

verb_relations.subject: that
verb_relations.sentence: In bone cells, primary cilia act as mechanosensors that respond to and flex extracellular fluid impulses generated by the body during walking and running.

verb_relations.subject: cell
verb_relations.sentence: When primary cilia bend, the increased tension on the membrane opens mechanosensitive ion channels, which leads to intracellular Ca2+ influx, membrane depolarization, and activation of nerve fibers and then, the cell experiences mechanical stimulation (Whitfield, 2003).

verb_relations.subject: environment
verb_relations.sentence: Studies have shown that a simulated microgravity environment eliminates the formation of primary cilia, inhibits the formation and mineralization of rat skull osteoblasts, and significantly shortens the residual cilia (Shi et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence:
3.1.1 Hypergravity and Osteoblasts
MC3T3-E1 cells as osteoblast precursors are commonly used for mechanical sensing and gravity studies, which often use different methods or stimulatory interventions to observe the effects of osteoblast differentiation.

verb_relations.subject: hypergravity
verb_relations.sentence: Similarly, hypergravity of 3 × g stimulates bone formation by enhancing the activity of osteopontin and RUNX2 in osteoblasts (Zhou et al., 2015).
Kawao et al. (Kawao et al., 2020) found increased mRNA levels of RUNX2, osterix, ALP, and osteocalcin; ALP activity; and mineralization in vivo in osteoblasts from mice exposed to 3 ×g hypergravity.

verb_relations.subject: levels
verb_relations.sentence: In addition, Woodcock et al. (Woodcock et al., 2019) found that high gravity effectively increased the intracellular viscosity of MC3T3-E1 cells and promoted the maturation and differentiation of osteoblasts, whereas higher levels (10, 15, and 20 × g) had a more significant effect.

verb_relations.subject: sclerostin
verb_relations.sentence: In space microgravity and simulated microgravity, sclerostin (SOST) enhances osteoclast formation by decreasing the production of osteoprotegerin (OPG) in osteoblasts and increasing the secretion of receptor activator of nuclear factor (NF)-κB ligand (RANKL) (Saxena et al., 2011; Chatani et al., 2016).

verb_relations.subject: microgravity
verb_relations.sentence: Similar studies have shown that microgravity reduced osteoblast production and enhanced that of osteoclasts by decreasing OPG secretion by osteoblasts (increasing the RANKL/OPG ratio) (Rucci et al., 2007).

verb_relations.subject: that
verb_relations.sentence: Serum glucocorticoid levels increased significantly on day 3 in an animal model of hindlimb unloading that simulated microgravity.

verb_relations.subject: levels
verb_relations.sentence: High glucocorticoid levels inhibited the expression of Wnt/β-catenin signaling pathway molecules and upregulated the expression of SOST in bone cells (Yang et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: factor
verb_relations.sentence: A recent study showed that the leukemia inhibitory factor (LIF) enhanced signal transducer and activator of transcription 3 (STAT3) phosphorylation in BMSCs and increased the expression of ALP and osteogenic genes.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: However, in the in vitro microgravity environment, the secretion of LIF by bone cells was inhibited, which weakened the osteogenic effect (Du et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: Microgravity
verb_relations.sentence: Microgravity also increases the level of oxidative damage markers in the body and weakens the total antioxidant capacity.

verb_relations.subject: species
verb_relations.sentence: Reactive oxygen species (ROS) inhibit the function of osteoblasts (Manolagas, 2010; Steller et al., 2018), promote MC3T3-E1 cell apoptosis, and downregulate the expression of MAF BZIP transcription factor G (MafG) (Wang et al., 2021b).

verb_relations.subject: microRNA
verb_relations.sentence: In addition, microRNA (miR)-494 inhibits BMP2-induced osteoblast differentiation by downregulating BMPR2 and RUNX2 under microgravity simulation (Qin et al., 2019).

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: Although resistance training for astronauts can effectively prevent bone loss (Stein, 2013), the adverse effects of the space microgravity environment on the bones of astronauts can last for several years, which highlights the importance of studying bone metabolism under microgravity conditions.

verb_relations.subject: cost
verb_relations.sentence: The huge cost of in vivo and in vitro experiments on space flight has necessitated the development of a variety of experimental platforms to simulate ground microgravity environments (Bonnefoy et al., 2021; Iordachescu et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: develop
verb_relations.sentence: However, fully replicating the changes induced by space microgravity exposure is challenging and, therefore, the develop of more reliable and accurate ground microgravity simulation environments for in-depth research is necessary.

verb_relations.subject: CCP
verb_relations.sentence: As a form of stress stimulated by mechanical stress, CCP is an effective condition for stimulating bone tissue growth.

verb_relations.subject: it
verb_relations.sentence: However, when CCP is too high or insufficient, it can reduce bone mass and lead to bone loss.

verb_relations.subject: CCP
verb_relations.sentence: Imamura et al. (Imamura et al., 1990) Found that CCP inhibits osteoblast differentiation of MC3T3-E1 cells through production of PGE2.

verb_relations.subject: study
verb_relations.sentence: Their further study found that applying continuous static pressure (3 atm, ATM) to MC3T3-E1 cells inhibited the ALP activity of the osteoblasts and promoted PGE2 secretion.

verb_relations.subject: stress
verb_relations.sentence: Xiaoqing Shen et al. (Shen et al., 2017) showed that compressive stress at the range of 5.0 g/cm2 had no significantly different effects on the survival rate of MC3T3-E1 cells.

verb_relations.subject: dose
verb_relations.sentence: These results indicate that CCP has a positive effect on the differentiation of MC3T3-E1 cells and osteoblasts, but the exact dose of CCP for MC3T3-E1 cells and specific effects are currently unclear.

Recently, Shu et al. (Somemura et al., 2021) reported that treating osteoblasts in a three-dimensional (3D) cell-collagen sponge construct with 25.5 gf/cm2 (2.5 kPa) for 24 h upregulated glucose transporter1 (Glut1), RUNX2 and ALP, whereas silent mating type information regulation 2 homolog 1 (sirtuin 1, SIRT1) was downregulated in osteoblasts induced by compressive mechanical loading.

verb_relations.subject: pathway
verb_relations.sentence: They hypothesized that the Glut1/SIRT1/RUNX2 pathway in osteoblasts may play a role in mechanical stress-induced bone formation and osteoblast differentiation.

verb_relations.subject: osteocytes
verb_relations.sentence: In vitro studies have repeatedly confirmed that osteocytes (not only osteoblasts, but also osteoblasts, osteoclasts and their progenitors) do indeed exhibit altered activity at hydrostatic pressures of up to 1 Hz, with amplitudes of tens (to hundreds) of kilopascals.

verb_relations.subject: that
verb_relations.sentence: With this problem in mind, the multi-scale mechanical biology method and multi-scale mechanical model proposed by Scheiner et al. that connect porous micromechanics and mathematical systems biology provide valuable insights for this problem (Scheiner et al., 2014; Scheiner et al., 2016; Estermann and Scheiner, 2018).

verb_relations.subject: types
verb_relations.sentence: Various types of stress can produce relative strain when applied to an object.

verb_relations.subject: Ilizarov
verb_relations.sentence: Ilizarov (Ilizarov, 1989) was the first to successfully apply traction stress to stimulate bone formation, and proposed the theory of “distraction osteogenesis”.

verb_relations.subject: that
verb_relations.sentence:
3.1.4.1 Tensile Strain and Osteoblasts
The tensile stress of osteoblasts cultured in vitro was mainly achieved by stretching the culture medium membrane that adhered to the osteoblasts.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: Furthermore, this process passively pulls osteoblasts that have adhered to the culture medium membrane, which simulates osteoblast stress in vivo.

verb_relations.subject: stretching
verb_relations.sentence: Chen et al. (Chen et al., 2018) found that mechanical stretching of human jaw bone marrow MSCs using the Flexcell tension system in vitro significantly increased ALP activity and calcium deposition.
Davidson et al. (Davidson et al., 2013) performed a mandibular osteotomy and pull experiment on Sprague-Dawley rats using a pull osteogenesis technique.

verb_relations.subject: it
verb_relations.sentence: In addition to mechanical tensile force acting directly on osteoblast precursors and osteoblasts, it can also induce osteogenesis indirectly by affecting other mechanosensitive cells.

Dong et al. (Dong et al., 2021) reported that macrophages are exposed to cyclic stretching with a 5% strain at a frequency of 1.0 Hz for up to 12 h using the FX-4000 Flexcell, and the stretched macrophages were co-cultured with BMSCs.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: The expression of opsin (OPN) and RUNX2 was significantly increased and induced Yes-associated protein (YAP) activation and nuclear translocation, which subsequently regulated downstream BMP2 expression to promote BMSCs osteogenesis (Dong et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: levels
verb_relations.sentence: Li et al.(2020b) induced distraction osteogenesis of osteoblasts, and found that their proliferation was enhanced and mRNA levels of ALP, RUNX2, osteocalcin (OCN), collagen type I, hypoxia-inducible factor (HIF)-1α and vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) were significantly increased.

verb_relations.subject: inflammation
verb_relations.sentence: However, in vivo studies showed that locally generated bacterial inflammation inhibited RUNX2 expression and up-regulated c-fos and interleukin (IL)-1β expression levels in osteoblasts induced by tensile strain, and reduced the osteogenesis of osteoblasts under continuous tensile stress (Lacey et al., 2009; Li et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: Obesity
verb_relations.sentence: Obesity induced by high-fat diet decreases osteoblast activity in alveolar bone of the OTM stretching side (Luo et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: it
verb_relations.sentence: Therefore, it is necessary to consider several factors to maximize the function of osteoblasts using tensile stress.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: Applying loads (including mechanical loads, muscle contractions, blood pressure, and lymphatic drainage) to the bones causes the interstitial fluid to flow, which compresses the lacunar-canalicular system, thereby inducing various mechanical stimuli including FSS (Duncan and Turner, 1995; Tanaka et al., 2005; Price et al., 2011).

verb_relations.subject: FSS
verb_relations.sentence: FSS can further induce changes in the biomechanical properties of osteoblasts.

verb_relations.subject: FSS
verb_relations.sentence: Previous studies have shown that FSS inhibits tumor necrosis factor (TNF)-α-induced osteoblast apoptosis (Pavalko et al., 2003).

verb_relations.subject: FSS
verb_relations.sentence: More recent studies have shown that FSS can activate the ERK5-serine-threonine protein kinase B (AKT)-forkhead box O3a (FoxO3a)-Bim/FasL signaling pathway, inhibit the activation of caspase 3, and protect osteoblasts from apoptosis induced by TNF-α (Bin et al., 2016).

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: LncRNA TUG1 upregulated fibroblast growth factor receptor 1 (FGFR1) expression by sponging miR-34a, which promoted osteoblast proliferation and inhibited osteoblast apoptosis (Wang et al., 2021e).

verb_relations.subject: FSS
verb_relations.sentence: This study also found that FSS downregulated miR-140-5p and promote osteoblast proliferation by activating the vascular endothelial growth factor-A (VEGFA)/ERK5 signaling pathway (Wang et al., 2021d).

verb_relations.subject: FSS
verb_relations.sentence: FSS increased the expression of Piezo1 in MC3T3-E1 cells, activated the AKT- serine-threonine protein kinase glycogen synthase kinase 3 (GSK3)/β-catenin pathway and upregulated the expression level of RUNX-2 (Song et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: them
verb_relations.sentence: FSS remodels the cytoskeleton of MC3T3-E1 cell; arranges F-actin proteins in one direction, making them more compact and uniform; and increases the expression level of phospho-paxillin and integrin-α5 (Jin et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: study
verb_relations.sentence: Recently, the in-depth study of bone ingrowth between internal fixation materials and bone interface has encouraged the study of material-bone interface-mechanical stimulation as an emerging focus.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: Lei et al.(2021) reported the effect of FSS on human MG-63 osteoblast-like cells on titanium with different surface modifications, which was particularly evident on the implant-bone interface.

verb_relations.subject: FAK
verb_relations.sentence: This study found that FSS (12 dyn/cm2) significantly induced cell proliferation and upregulated the expression level of focal adhesion kinase (FAK), which the authors speculated that FAK may play a key role in the mechanical transduction of the implant-bone interface (Lei et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: FAK is a nonreceptor tyrosine kinase, which plays a key role in integrin-mediated signal transduction and downstream signaling pathways (Tapial Martinez et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: FSS
verb_relations.sentence: Wang J. et al. (Wang J. et al., 2021) found that the interface force or adsorption force of protein-material can regulate the organization of the actin cytoskeleton and promote the formation of focal adhesion, and that FSS promotes assembly of the actin cytoskeleton and decomposition of focal adhesions.

verb_relations.subject: studies
verb_relations.sentence: These emerging studies will contribute to the designing of a harmonious bioreactor and mechanical load to facilitate the comprehensive study of bone tissue regeneration.

verb_relations.subject: load
verb_relations.sentence:
4 Signal Pathways Mediating Effects of Mechanical Stress Stimulation on Osteoblasts
Mechanical load induces stress stimulation of the bone and transmits the force to the bone cells.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: The receptors on the cell membrane are activated by binding of specific ligands, which triggers the signal cascade and enhances expression of downstream target genes, playing a role in bone metabolism.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: Exposure of the transmembrane co-receptors low-density lipoprotein (LDL) receptor related protein 5 (Lrp5), Lrp6, and Frizzled (FZD) protein family to Wnt, activates the Lrp/FZD receptor complex on the cell surface, which inhibits the degradation activity of β-Catenin through the phosphorylation of downstream protein kinases.

verb_relations.subject: catenin
verb_relations.sentence: Subsequently, the stably accumulated β-catenin in the cytoplasm enters the nucleus and combines with the T-cell factor/lymphoid enhancer factor (TCF/LEF) transcription factor family to initiate the transcription of downstream target genes to promote the expression of osteogenic genes (such as OPG) and promote bone formation.

verb_relations.subject: mice
verb_relations.sentence: Studies have found that mice with Lrp5 deletion mutations continue to show a state of low bone mass (Sawakami et al., 2006; Iwaniec et al., 2007).

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: More recent studies showed that human patients with Lrp5 missense mutation (A745V) had severe osteoporosis, which may be attributable to the weakening of the anabolic response of bones to mechanical stress (Kiel et al., 2007; Norwitz et al., 2019).

verb_relations.subject: deletion
verb_relations.sentence: In addition, under mechanical load, heterozygous deletion of β-catenin in a single osteocyte in mice eliminates the bone synthesis response to mechanical load and the ability to form new bone (Javaheri et al., 2014).

verb_relations.subject: studies
verb_relations.sentence: Numerous studies using in vitro and in vitro models have also demonstrated the involvement of the Wnt pathway in mechanical stress (Bonewald and Johnson, 2008; Duan and Bonewald, 2016; Kang et al., 2016; Fu et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: SOST
verb_relations.sentence: However, SOST negatively regulates the Wnt/β -catenin pathway (Choi and Robling, 2021).

verb_relations.subject: load
verb_relations.sentence: The mechanical load can inhibit the expression of DKK1 in osteocytes and promote the upregulation of Wnt signaling (Holguin et al., 2016).

verb_relations.subject: studies
verb_relations.sentence: In vivo studies using hypergravity (3 × g) showed significantly reduced DKK2 levels in the serum and DKK2 mRNA levels in the soleus muscle of mice (Kawao et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: mechanism
verb_relations.sentence: Regulation of the Wnt pathway has a positive effect on compensatory mechanisms for coping with mechanical stress and changing bone mass, but the specific mechanism remains to be further studied.

verb_relations.subject: pathway
verb_relations.sentence: The Notch signaling pathway, which is expressed in almost all organ systems, is highly conserved and plays an important role in the occurrence and development of diseases by regulating various cell processes (Andersson et al., 2011).

verb_relations.subject: types
verb_relations.sentence: Mammals have multiple Notch receptors, and the common types are Notch 1, Notch 2, Notch 3, and Notch-4.

verb_relations.subject: Binding
verb_relations.sentence: Binding of the specific ligand to the receptor activates the Notch signaling pathway and its downstream target genes [including hes family bHLH transcription factor 1 (Hes1), hes related family bHLH transcription factor with YRPW motif 1 (Hey1), and Hey2] are transcribed.

verb_relations.subject: Overexpression
verb_relations.sentence: Overexpression of NICD1 in osteocytes reduces bone resorption, leading to an increase in bone mass.

verb_relations.subject: activation
verb_relations.sentence: Conversely, specific activation of Notch signaling in immature osteoblasts impaired their differentiation, leading to osteopenia (Canalis et al., 2013).

verb_relations.subject: stress
verb_relations.sentence: Wang L. et al. (Wang et al., 2020a) found that mechanical stretch stress can activate the Notch1 signaling pathway and the expression of ALP, RUNX2, OCN, bone sialoprotein (BSP), and promote the osteogenic differentiation of human periodontal ligament stem cells.

Ziouti et al. (Ziouti et al., 2019) found that applying tibial cyclic compression load (216 cycles at 4 Hz, peak strains at a tibial midshaft of +900 με) to wild-type mice induced specific Notch target genes (mRNA expression of Hes1, Hey1, and Hey2).

verb_relations.subject: stretching
verb_relations.sentence: Cyclic stretching of primary human BMSCs increased the gene expression of Notch receptors Notch1 and Notch2 by more than 60-fold and 30-fold, respectively.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: ERK, which belongs to the mitogen-activated protein kinases (MAPKs) family, has strong catalytic activity and plays a key role in upstream signal transduction pathways including mechanical signals in cellular reactions (Fey et al., 2012; Yuan et al., 2020).

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: Studies have shown that loading MC3T3-E1 cells with FSS increases cyclooxygenase (COX)-2 activity, which activates osteoblast-like cell proliferation and anabolic metabolism (Bo et al., 2016; Ding et al., 2019).

verb_relations.subject: Knockout
verb_relations.sentence: Knockout of ERK5 using small interfering RNA (siRNA) prevented FSS from upregulating COX-2 and cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP) (Jiang et al., 2015).

verb_relations.subject: downregulation
verb_relations.sentence: Wang X. et al. found that FSS-induced downregulation of miR-140-5p regulates ERK5 signal activation through VEGFA and promotes osteoblast proliferation (Wang et al., 2021d).

verb_relations.subject: FSS
verb_relations.sentence: FSS inhibits caspase 3 activation to prevent osteoblast apoptosis by activating the ERK5-AKT-Foxo3a pathway in MC3T3-E1 cells (Bin et al., 2016).

verb_relations.subject: effect
verb_relations.sentence: Other studies have shown that the effect of mechanical load on ERK5 is an important factor affecting the proliferation of osteoblasts (Li et al., 2012; Zhang et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: ones
verb_relations.sentence: The mammalian genome encodes about 20 kinds of Rho gtpase, and currently the most researched ones are RhoA, Rac1 and Cdc42 (Ridley and Hall, 1992).

verb_relations.subject: adhesions
verb_relations.sentence: When exogenous forces or forces generated by the cell itself and the cytoskeleton act on the cell (Chen, 2008), cell surface adhesions, cytoskeleton and membrane tension work together to affect mechanosensors and stimulate mechanical responses (Vogel and Sheetz, 2006; Hoffman et al., 2011).

verb_relations.subject: ROCK
verb_relations.sentence: Studies have shown that Rho/ROCK converts mechanical stimulation into gene expression changes through the actin-myocardin-related transcription factor (MRTF)-serum response factor (SRF) pathway (Zhao et al., 2007).

verb_relations.subject: stimulation
verb_relations.sentence: Dupont et al. (Dupont et al., 2011) found that stretch stimulation can activate the RhoA/ROCK signaling pathway and YAP/transcriptional co-activator with PDZ-binding motif (TAZ), promote bone formation, inhibit adipogenesis, and form actin at the same time.

verb_relations.subject: RhoA
verb_relations.sentence: Similarly, RhoA feels high ECM stiffness through focal adhesion, promotes actin polymerization and stress fiber formation, transmits stiffness signals to YAP/TAZ, and regulates the sensitivity of osteoblasts (Wagh et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: RhoA
verb_relations.sentence: Studies have been found that RhoA and its effector protein ROCKII regulate the differentiation of C3H10T1/2 cells induced by oscillatory fluid flow to osteogenic differentiation.

verb_relations.subject: RhoA
verb_relations.sentence: At the same time, activated RhoA and fluid flow have an additive effect on the expression of RUNX2 (Arnsdorf et al., 2009).

verb_relations.subject: it
verb_relations.sentence: The RhoA signalling pathway is central to mechanotransduction because it plays a key role in regulating the catin cytoskeleton and its response to mechanical force.

verb_relations.subject: pathways
verb_relations.sentence: In addition, other signaling pathways such as the transforming growth factor-β (TGFβ)-Smad (Zou et al., 2021), NF-κB (Wang et al., 2015), and BMP(da Silva Madaleno et al., 2020; Wei et al., 2020), signaling pathways also have an important role in the responses of osteoblast-like cells under mechanical stimulation, and there is crosstalk between various signaling pathways (Kopf et al., 2014; Grafe et al., 2018; Shuai et al., 2018).

verb_relations.subject: effects
verb_relations.sentence: Moreover, the effects of mechanical stimulation on the various signal pathways mediated the response of osteoblasts are interactive and related (Table 1).

verb_relations.subject: it
verb_relations.sentence: Once mechanical stimulus receptors are affected by mechanical loading and unloading, it is possible to activate multiple biological signaling pathways during the conversion of physical signals into chemical stimulation signals, thus generates positive or negative regulation of osteoblast cells.

verb_relations.subject: research
verb_relations.sentence: Future research should continue to focus on clarifying the signal pathways mediating the mechanism underlying the actions of mechanical stimulation on osteoblast-like cells.

verb_relations.subject: Loading
verb_relations.sentence:
TABLE 1.
5 Conclusion
Loading and unloading mechanical stress affects the proliferation, differentiation, and function of osteoblast-like cells.

verb_relations.subject: which
verb_relations.sentence: Studies have shown that osteoblast-like cells, which are sensitive to mechanical stimulation, are the basic cell model for studying the processes of bone growth, development, and formation (Siddiqui and Partridge, 2016; Thomas and Jaganathan, 2021).

verb_relations.subject: perception
verb_relations.sentence: The perception of mechanical stress by osteoblasts first involves actions on target genes through various pathways such as Ca2+, Piezo1, ECM-integrin-cytoskeleton, and cell regulatory factors.

verb_relations.subject: Mechanoreceptor
verb_relations.sentence: Mechanoreceptor also regulates the expression of corresponding genes to convert mechanical stimulus signals to chemical stimuli, and then regulates various receptors on the cell membrane, cytoplasm, and nucleus through chemoreceptors to regulate the bone formation mechanism (Steward and Kelly, 2015; Augat et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: studies
verb_relations.sentence: Number experimental studies have investigated osteogenic effects under mechanical stress (Chermside-Scabbo et al., 2020; Eichholz et al., 2020; Jeon et al., 2021; Lei et al., 2021).

verb_relations.subject: influence
verb_relations.sentence: The influence of different mechanical forces on bone tissue produces various effects.

verb_relations.subject: methods
verb_relations.sentence: Most previous studies used a single mechanical stimulus on a single mechanosensitive cell, and the mechanical loading methods and mechanical devices used did not have a relatively unified standard.

verb_relations.subject: results
verb_relations.sentence: The results obtained from studies using randomly selected or combinations of different mechanical stimuli were no systematic and lacked standardization and accuracy.

verb_relations.subject: designs
verb_relations.sentence: Consequently, designs of bone metabolism studies need to include a more suitable mechanical experimental environment to accurately control the mechanical parameters of mechanical stress stimulation, such as the type, duration, intensity, and cycle of mechanical stimulation).

verb_relations.subject: studies
verb_relations.sentence: Furthermore, such studies should explore the influence of the body on mechanical stimulation.

verb_relations.subject: this
verb_relations.sentence: It is gratifying that mechanism-driven biology and biochemistry have been put into mathematical systems biology formats, this will certainly lead the research field to a more precise direction (Pastrama et al., 2018; Lavaill et al., 2020; Larcher and Scheiner, 2021).

verb_relations.subject: This
verb_relations.sentence: This would provide an important theoretical basis for clinical treatment of bone diseases with a focus on issues such as bone health, fracture healing, and bone ingrowth at the bone-material interface.

verb_relations.subject: experiments
verb_relations.sentence: Further in vivo experiments need to study the changes in macroscopic bone tissue metabolic activity under stress stimulation, whereas future in vitro experiments should explore the specific mechanisms and signal transduction pathways mediating the effects of different mechanical stimuli on various mechanosensitive cells of bone tissue.
